_comment: Pterodactyl egg: a discord.py bot instrumented with argus-dpy. Import via Panel -> Nests -> Import Egg. Argus auto-detects SERVER_PORT, so /metrics binds your primary allocation with no extra config; set the ARGUS_* variables for auth or push.
name: discord.py bot (Argus)
author: callmeSage0@proton.me
description: A Python discord.py bot with argus-dpy observability. Serves /metrics + dashboard on the primary allocation, or pushes via OTLP/Fleet with no inbound port.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
cd /mnt/server
# Install argus-dpy plus the dotenv extra (so an uploaded .env is auto-loaded)
# and the otlp extra (for push). Plus your own requirements.txt if present.
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install "argus-dpy[dotenv,otlp]"
if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
echo "install complete"

variables.name: Bot file
variables.description: The Python file Pterodactyl runs to start your bot.

variables.name: Discord token
variables.description: Your bot token from the Discord Developer Portal. Treat as a secret.

variables.name: Dashboard auth token
variables.description: Gates the dashboard + /api when the port is publicly reachable. Leave blank only on a private/loopback allocation.

variables.name: OTLP endpoint (push, optional)
variables.description: Push metrics to an OTLP collector / Grafana Cloud instead of being scraped. No inbound port needed.

variables.name: Fleet URL (push, optional)
variables.description: Report to an Argus Fleet control plane for a single multi-bot pane. Outbound-only.

variables.name: Fleet token (push, optional)
variables.description: The token for the Fleet control plane referenced above.
